vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.11.1
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1487: feat(flywheel): substrate-bug provenance + telemetry for v1.0 readiness criteria

plan.title: feat(flywheel): substrate-bug provenance + telemetry for v1.0 readiness criteria
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: Without substrate-bug provenance and per-run telemetry, none of the seven v1.0 readiness criteria in FLYWHEEL-VISION.md can be measured. Today: (a) substrate bugs filed by an agent during a Flywheel pass are indistinguishable from operator-asked-agent filings (same author, same shape), so we can't compute 'agent-discovered substrate bug rate'; (b) per-issue stage timing, intervention count, pass count, and outcome are scattered across the events table with no aggregator, so we can't compute pass-rate, MTTR, intervention-rate, time-consistency, or flake-rate. Everything else in the v1.0 vision is aspirational until this lands.
narratives.Proposal: Three layers: (1) Provenance — Cloister threads PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_RUN_ID into every agent spawned while a Flywheel run is active; a Claude Code PreToolUse Bash hook intercepts `gh issue create` and appends a trailer block (Flywheel-Run-Id, Flywheel-Filed-By, Flywheel-Discovered-In) to the issue body. (2) Telemetry events + storage — new SubstrateBugFiledEvent and OperatorInterventionEvent domain events, plus a flywheel_substrate_bugs SQLite projection populated by a 60s GitHub poller. (3) Aggregation + surfaces — flywheel-telemetry service derives the 7 criteria from events + substrate bugs over a 30-day rolling window; GET /api/flywheel/stats route, `pan flywheel stats` CLI, and a new Stats tab on FlywheelPage render each criterion as value-vs-target with green/yellow/red status.

autoDecisions.summary: Filed-By: agent only when flywheel-orchestrator itself filed; operator-via-agent → operator.
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the PRD's 'operator asks agent to file → operator' rule and is the only classification we can detect reliably without explicit intent signaling.

autoDecisions.summary: Trailer block (not labels) as provenance carrier.
autoDecisions.rationale: Labels can be edited post-hoc; the initial issue body is immutable for forensic purposes. Matches the PRD-proposed format verbatim.
Agents may invoke `/usr/bin/gh` with a full path, bypassing a shim. PreToolUse runs unconditionally regardless of how the agent spelled the command.

autoDecisions.summary: Stats UI placement = third tab on FlywheelPage left pane (Status / State / Stats).
autoDecisions.rationale: Tab pattern already exists in the page header; adding a third tab is minimal incremental UI surface and preserves the operator-comment requirement to not crowd primary outputs.

autoDecisions.summary: Substrate-attributable failure = review/test failure followed within 24h by a substrate bug with Flywheel-Discovered-In pointing at this issue.
autoDecisions.rationale: Conservative: misses substrate failures that were never filed (rare in practice) but never falsely blames code-defect failures.

autoDecisions.summary: Complexity buckets: simple=1-3 beads, medium=4-8, complex=9+.
autoDecisions.rationale: Beads count is the only post-planning complexity signal we have; bucket boundaries are heuristic and tunable without schema changes.

autoDecisions.summary: 30-day rolling window default, --window arg accepts Go-style duration.
autoDecisions.rationale: 30 days matches the FLYWHEEL-VISION readiness-criteria window verbatim.
Explicitly out-of-scope in the issue body.

autoDecisions.summary: messageAgent emits operator.intervention only for user-source callers (pan tell, dashboard message), not internal automation callers (review nudges, poke).
autoDecisions.rationale: Internal callers would inflate intervention count and break criterion #5; the existing caller string distinguishes them.

items.title: Thread PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_RUN_ID + AGENT_ROLE into every agent spawn

narrative.Action: In spawnRun() (src/lib/agents.ts) read getFlywheelActiveRunId() from app_settings at spawn time. If a run id is present and validates as RUN-<digits>, set PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_RUN_ID and PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_AGENT_ROLE=<role> in the spawned process env. spawnFlywheelAgent (src/lib/cloister/flywheel.ts) sets both unconditionally for the orchestrator since it owns the run.

subItems.title: spawnRun sets PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_RUN_ID when getFlywheelActiveRunId() returns a value at spawn time
spawnRun sets PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_AGENT_ROLE to the role string passed to it (work|plan|review|test|ship|flywheel)
spawnFlywheelAgent always sets both env vars for the flywheel-orchestrator agent (with role=flywheel)
Unit tests cover (a) Flywheel run active → env vars set; (b) no active run → env vars NOT set; (c) orchestrator always has both vars
agents.ts changes use async fs only (no execSync/readFileSync) per CLAUDE.md dashboard-server rules

items.title: gh-issue-trailer-hook: PreToolUse hook that appends Flywheel-* trailers to gh issue create body

narrative.Action:
Create scripts/hooks/gh-issue-trailer-hook.ts (compiled to bin/) that reads Claude Code PreToolUse JSON payload from stdin. If tool_name=='Bash' and the command is `gh issue create` (recognize flags --title/--body/--body-file), parse out the body, append '

---
Flywheel-Run-Id: <PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_RUN_ID>
Flywheel-Filed-By: <agent|operator>
Flywheel-Discovered-In: <PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID's issueId>
' to it, and emit modifiedToolInput JSON. Filed-By=agent ONLY when PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_AGENT_ROLE=='flywheel'; everything else → operator. Install via pan admin hooks install (existing infra).

subItems.title: Hook handles `gh issue create --title X --body Y` and `--body-file path` and stdin-based body input; injects trailer correctly in each shape
Idempotent: if body already contains a 'Flywheel-Run-Id:' line, hook returns input unchanged
Non-Bash tools and non-issue-create Bash invocations pass through unchanged (emit empty `{}` and exit 0)
PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_AGENT_ROLE=='flywheel' produces Filed-By: agent; any other role (or unset) produces Filed-By: operator
Discovered-In resolves from PANOPTICON_AGENT_ID's state.json issueId field; if unresolvable, line is omitted (not left blank)
Unit tests cover all 6 above cases via stdin/stdout JSON shape; tests use vi.useFakeTimers if any retry/timer logic is added
Hook is installable via `pan admin hooks install` (registers into the standard host hooks directory)

items.title: Wire gh-issue-trailer-hook into roles/flywheel.md, roles/work.md, roles/plan.md PreToolUse Bash matchers

narrative.Action: Append a new PreToolUse Bash hook entry referencing $HOME/.panopticon/bin/gh-issue-trailer-hook to the existing matcher arrays in roles/flywheel.md, roles/work.md, roles/plan.md. Order it BEFORE rtk-bash-filter so the rewritten command is what later hooks see. Do not remove or reorder existing hooks.

subItems.title: All three role files (flywheel, work, plan) have the trailer hook in their PreToolUse Bash matcher
Existing hooks (pre-tool-hook, rtk-bash-filter, heartbeat-hook, etc.) are preserved verbatim — only addition, no edits or reorders
Trailer hook entry appears before rtk-bash-filter in the array
Snapshot test asserts the full PreToolUse Bash matcher order for each of the three role files

items.title: Add SubstrateBugFiledEvent to @panctl/contracts DomainEvent union

narrative.Action: In packages/contracts/src/events.ts add SubstrateBugFiledEvent Schema.Struct with type='substrate.bug_filed', payload={issueId, runId?, filedBy: 'agent'|'operator', discoveredIn?: issueId, severity: 'P0'|'P1'|'P2'}. Add to DomainEvent union. Export type.

subItems.title: SubstrateBugFiledEvent matches the contract shape above and decodes/encodes round-trip
Added to DomainEvent union and to event-reducers.ts as a no-op reducer (event is read-only telemetry, no state mutation)
Schema test covers happy-path decode + decode with missing optional fields

items.title: Add OperatorInterventionEvent to @panctl/contracts DomainEvent union

narrative.Action: In packages/contracts/src/events.ts add OperatorInterventionEvent Schema.Struct with type='operator.intervention', payload={issueId, kind: 'tell'|'pause'|'restart'|'manual_edit'|'deep_wipe'|'unpause'|'untroubled', source: string}. Add to DomainEvent union. Export type.

subItems.title: OperatorInterventionEvent matches contract shape; kind is a closed enum literal
Added to DomainEvent union and reducers (no-op)
Schema test covers decode of each kind variant

items.title: SQLite migration v44: flywheel_substrate_bugs table + repository

narrative.Action: In src/lib/database/schema.ts add migration v44 creating table `flywheel_substrate_bugs (issue_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, filed_at TEXT NOT NULL, run_id TEXT, filed_by TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (filed_by IN ('agent','operator')), discovered_in_issue_id TEXT, severity TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'P2', status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open', fix_merged_at TEXT, fix_commit_sha TEXT, updated_at TEXT NOT NULL)`. Index on (filed_at), (filed_by, filed_at), (status, fix_merged_at). Create src/lib/database/flywheel-substrate-bugs-db.ts with upsert, getByIssueId, listInWindow(since), markFixed(issueId, commitSha, mergedAt) repository methods using Effect.runSync wrappers.

subItems.title: Migration v44 creates the table with the exact columns above and three indexes
Migration is idempotent (re-run on a v44+ database is a no-op)
Repository module exports upsert / getByIssueId / listInWindow / markFixed with typed return values
Unit tests cover upsert (insert + update), listInWindow filter by filed_at range, markFixed sets status='fixed' + fix_merged_at + fix_commit_sha
filed_by CHECK constraint rejects values other than 'agent' or 'operator'

items.title: Emit operator.intervention from messageAgent (pan tell + dashboard message)

narrative.Action: In src/lib/agents.ts messageAgent and the dashboard message route, after a successful message delivery, emit OperatorInterventionEvent{issueId: derivedFromAgentState, kind:'tell', source: callerString} via the event store. Only emit when the caller string matches a user-source whitelist ('pan-tell', 'dashboard:user-message'); skip for internal callers ('review:nudge', 'automation:poke').

subItems.title: Calling messageAgent with caller='pan-tell' emits an OperatorInterventionEvent with kind='tell'
Calling messageAgent with an internal caller (e.g. 'review:nudge') does NOT emit an intervention event
issueId on the emitted event is resolved from the target agent's state.json; if state has no issueId, the event is skipped (with a debug log)
Tests cover both emission and skip paths via mocked event store

items.title: Emit operator.intervention from pause/restart/deep-wipe agent endpoints

narrative.Action: In the dashboard routes for pause (/api/agents/:id/pause), restart (/api/agents/:id/restart), and deep-wipe (/api/agents/:id/deep-wipe), emit OperatorInterventionEvent with kind='pause'/'restart'/'deep_wipe' and source='dashboard'. Also instrument the CLI commands pan pause, pan restart, pan wipe so they emit when invoked from the user's terminal.

subItems.title: Pause / restart / deep-wipe HTTP routes each emit exactly one OperatorInterventionEvent on success
Failed requests (404, validation error) emit NO intervention event
CLI variants (pan pause, pan kill, pan wipe) emit the same events via dashboard internal-token POST
Route tests assert event payload matches expected shape per kind

items.title: Emit operator.intervention from unpause/untroubled + workspace file-edit endpoints

narrative.Action: Emit OperatorInterventionEvent with kind='unpause'/'untroubled'/'manual_edit' from pan unpause, pan untroubled, and the workspace file-edit dashboard endpoint (if it exists; if not, skip 'manual_edit' for v1 and document in hazards).

subItems.title: pan unpause and pan untroubled emit kind='unpause' / 'untroubled' events
If a workspace-file-edit dashboard endpoint exists, it emits kind='manual_edit'; otherwise that path is explicitly documented as not yet instrumented and a follow-up TODO is added in docs/FLYWHEEL.md

items.title: GitHub poller projects new substrate bugs into flywheel_substrate_bugs + emits SubstrateBugFiledEvent

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/server/services/substrate-bug-poller.ts: poll every 60s for GitHub issues authored by panopticon-agent[bot] OR labelled 'substrate' created/updated in the last 5 minutes. For each, parse the trailer block from the body (regex over Flywheel-Run-Id, Flywheel-Filed-By, Flywheel-Discovered-In lines). Upsert into flywheel_substrate_bugs (idempotent via PRIMARY KEY). On first insert, emit SubstrateBugFiledEvent. Severity from labels (P0/P1/P2 → severity, default P2). Also watch for merged PRs with 'fix(es)? #NNNN' / 'close(s|d)? #NNNN' / 'resolve(s|d)? #NNNN' referencing a substrate bug; if found, call markFixed(issueId, commitSha, mergedAt) to populate MTTR. Register the poller in src/dashboard/server/main.ts boot sequence, respecting the rate_limits table.

subItems.title: Poller runs every 60s while dashboard server is up, no execSync/readFileSync (Node.js event-loop safe)
Trailer regex correctly extracts runId, filedBy, discoveredIn from the canonical trailer block; handles missing lines gracefully
Idempotent: poller re-seeing the same issue does NOT emit a duplicate SubstrateBugFiledEvent (verified by emit count after running poller twice on same fixture)
PR-merge scan correctly resolves closes/fixes/resolves references and calls markFixed only for issues present in flywheel_substrate_bugs
Severity is read from labels (P0/P1/P2); absent → default P2; tests cover all four cases
Poller respects existing rate_limits table — backs off when GitHub returns 403 with X-RateLimit-Remaining=0
Vitest tests for poller use vi.useFakeTimers + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync — never real setTimeout-based delays (per CLAUDE.md test rules)

items.title: derivePipelineRunMetrics: per-issue stage durations from events table

narrative.Action: Add src/dashboard/server/services/pipeline-run-metrics.ts exporting derivePipelineRunMetrics(issueId): {plan, work, review, test, ship, mergeMs, outcome, interventionCount, passCount, headSha?} computed from the existing `events` table. Plan: agent.created(role=plan) → agent.completed(role=plan). Work: agent.created(role=work) → first pipeline.review-started. Review: pipeline.review-started → pipeline.review-completed (sum across cycles). Test: pipeline.test-started → pipeline.test-completed (sum). Ship: last passing review/test → merge-complete-event. PassCount: count of pipeline.review-completed{passed:false} + pipeline.test-completed{passed:false} for this issue. InterventionCount: count of operator.intervention events for this issue. Outcome: merged|parked|cancelled from issue.closed/issue.status_changed.

subItems.title: derivePipelineRunMetrics returns correct stage durations for a happy-path single-pass issue
Multi-cycle review/test correctly sums durations across cycles
passCount counts review+test failed completions
interventionCount counts operator.intervention events scoped to this issueId
outcome resolves to merged|parked|cancelled from terminal event; in-flight issues return outcome='in_flight' and are excluded from window-bound aggregates
All SQL queries are parameterized; no string concatenation of issueId into SQL

items.title: flywheel-telemetry service scaffold: 7-criterion stats interface returning placeholders

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/server/services/flywheel-telemetry.ts exporting computeFlywheelStats(window: Duration): Promise<FlywheelStats>. Returns the full FlywheelStats shape (defined in stats-contracts bead) with each of seven criteria as {value, target, status, trend, sampleSize, dataSufficient}. Initial implementation returns dataSufficient=false placeholder rows for all 7. Subsequent beads (telemetry-criterion-N-*) each replace one placeholder with a real computation. Each criterion has its own private helper so beads stay independently mergeable.

subItems.title: computeFlywheelStats(window) returns a structurally complete FlywheelStats with all 7 criteria populated (placeholder rows allowed)
Window arg accepts Go-style duration strings (30d, 7d, 24h, 1h)
Each criterion is computed by a separate exported helper function so subsequent beads can implement them in isolation
When fewer than 3 completed pipeline runs exist in the window, every criterion returns dataSufficient=false (per D11)
Snapshot test on the placeholder response pins the shape so frontend can render against it

items.title: Criterion 1: substrate-bug discovery rate (agent-filed bugs / pipeline runs)

narrative.Action: Implement computeCriterion1 in flywheel-telemetry.ts: numerator = COUNT(*) from flywheel_substrate_bugs WHERE filed_by='agent' AND filed_at BETWEEN since AND now; denominator = COUNT(DISTINCT issue_id) of completed pipeline runs in same window. Target: <2%. Status green if <2%, yellow 2-5%, red >5%.

subItems.title: value = agentBugsCount / completedRunsCount, returned as a fraction (e.g. 0.018 = 1.8%)
target=0.02, status thresholds: green<0.02, yellow 0.02-0.05, red>0.05
Test with fixture: 100 runs + 1 agent-filed substrate bug → value=0.01, status=green
Operator-filed bugs are EXCLUDED from numerator (only agent-discovered counts)

items.title: Criterion 2: critical/P0 substrate bug count

narrative.Action: Implement computeCriterion2: COUNT(*) FROM flywheel_substrate_bugs WHERE severity='P0' AND filed_at BETWEEN since AND now. Target: 0. Status green if 0, red if >=1.

subItems.title: value = count of P0 substrate bugs in window
target=0; status=green when value=0, red when value>=1
Test fixture: 1 P0 → red; 0 P0 → green

items.title: Criterion 3: pipeline pass success rate (substrate-attributable failures only)

narrative.Action: Implement computeCriterion3: 1 - (substrate-attributable failures / total review/test attempts). Substrate-attributable failure = pipeline.{review,test}-completed{passed:false} where within 24h after the failure event, a flywheel_substrate_bugs row was created with discovered_in_issue_id=issueId. Target: ≥99%. Status green ≥0.99, yellow 0.95-0.99, red <0.95.

subItems.title: Numerator counts ONLY substrate-attributable failures (per D13); legitimate code-defect failures are excluded
Denominator = total review attempts + test attempts in window
Target=0.99; green>=0.99, yellow [0.95, 0.99), red <0.95
Test fixture: 100 attempts, 1 substrate-attributable failure → 0.99 → green

items.title: Criterion 4: MTTR for filed substrate bugs (median + p95)

narrative.Action: Implement computeCriterion4: from flywheel_substrate_bugs WHERE status='fixed' AND fix_merged_at BETWEEN since AND now, compute durations = fix_merged_at - filed_at. Report {medianMs, p95Ms}. Target: median<24h, p95<7d. Status green if both met, yellow if median<24h but p95<14d, red otherwise.

subItems.title: value = {medianMs, p95Ms} computed from fix_merged_at - filed_at across all fixed bugs in window
Status green if median<24h AND p95<7d; yellow if median<24h AND p95<14d; red otherwise
If fewer than 3 fixed bugs in window → dataSufficient=false
Test fixture: 5 fixed bugs with known fix_merged_at - filed_at yields correct median + p95

items.title: Criterion 5: operator intervention rate per pipeline run

narrative.Action: Implement computeCriterion5: numerator = COUNT(*) operator.intervention events for issues whose pipeline run completed in window; denominator = COUNT(DISTINCT issueId) of completed runs in window. Value = numerator/denominator (interventions per run, NOT a fraction). Target: <5% — interpreted as <0.05 interventions/run on average (since 'rate per run' is the headline framing). Status green <0.05, yellow 0.05-0.20, red >0.20. UAT is explicitly NOT counted (no event emitted for UAT).

subItems.title: value = total interventions / completed runs in window
target=0.05; thresholds green<0.05, yellow [0.05,0.20], red>0.20
Fixture: 10 runs, 1 'tell' event total → value=0.10 → yellow
UAT actions (whatever UAT events are recorded) do NOT contribute to numerator

items.title: Criterion 6: time-in-pipeline consistency per complexity bucket (p95 <= 2x median)

narrative.Action: Implement computeCriterion6: for each completed run in window, sum stage durations (plan+work+review+test+ship+merge) to get totalMs. Bucket by beads count: simple=1-3, medium=4-8, complex=9+. For each bucket compute median + p95 of totalMs; check p95/median ratio. Return {simple: {medianMs, p95Ms, ratio, status}, medium: {...}, complex: {...}}. Target per bucket: ratio<=2.0. Status: green if all three buckets <=2.0, yellow if any bucket 2-3, red if any bucket >3.

subItems.title: Bucket assignment uses beads count from .beads/issues.jsonl OR (fallback) plan_items count from the vBRIEF spec
Per-bucket median + p95 computed correctly; ratio = p95/median
Buckets with fewer than 3 runs are marked dataSufficient=false, not red
Headline status = worst-bucket status (green if all <=2.0, yellow if any 2-3, red if any >3)
Test fixture: 6 runs across 2 buckets with known totalMs distribution produces correct ratios

items.title: Criterion 7: flake rate on substrate-attributable failures

narrative.Action: Implement computeCriterion7: flake = a review or test that PASSED on one cycle and FAILED on the next within the same pipeline run with no intervening commit (same head SHA). Per H9 hazard. Numerator = flake count; denominator = total substrate-attributable failures. Target: <5%. Status green <0.05, yellow 0.05-0.10, red >0.10.

subItems.title: Flake detection correctly identifies same-issue, same-head-SHA review/test with divergent outcomes across cycles
Numerator excludes failures where commit changed between cycles (legitimate fix attempts, not flakes)
target=0.05; green<0.05, yellow [0.05,0.10], red>0.10
Test fixture: same SHA pass-then-fail produces 1 flake; pass-fix-pass-fail across different SHAs produces 0 flakes

items.title: Add FlywheelStats schema to @panctl/contracts

narrative.Action: Add packages/contracts/src/flywheel-stats.ts exporting FlywheelStats Schema.Struct: { window: string, generatedAt: string, criteria: { c1_bugRate: FlywheelStatsCriterion, c2_p0Bugs: FlywheelStatsCriterion, c3_passRate: FlywheelStatsCriterion, c4_mttr: FlywheelStatsCriterion, c5_intervention: FlywheelStatsCriterion, c6_timeConsistency: FlywheelStatsCriterion, c7_flake: FlywheelStatsCriterion } }. FlywheelStatsCriterion = { label, value (number | object), target (number | object), status: 'green'|'yellow'|'red'|'insufficient_data', trend?: 'up'|'down'|'flat', sampleSize: number, dataSufficient: boolean }. Export TypeScript types and the decoder. Re-export from contracts/src/index.ts.

subItems.title: FlywheelStats Schema.Struct matches the shape above and round-trips through decode/encode
All 7 criteria keys are present (c1_..c7_) and each has the FlywheelStatsCriterion shape
FlywheelStatsCriterion.value supports both scalar (number) and object (e.g. {medianMs, p95Ms} for criterion 4) via Schema.Union
Types re-exported from packages/contracts/src/index.ts
Snapshot test pins the JSON shape so frontend + CLI can decode against it

items.title: GET /api/flywheel/stats?window=30d route

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/routes/flywheel.ts add a GET /api/flywheel/stats handler. Parse ?window= (default '30d'), call computeFlywheelStats(window), return FlywheelStats JSON. Validate response with the FlywheelStats schema before sending.

subItems.title: GET /api/flywheel/stats with no query returns 30-day stats
GET /api/flywheel/stats?window=7d returns 7-day stats
Invalid window (e.g. ?window=abc) returns 400 with error body
Response is validated against the FlywheelStats schema before send (catches accidental shape drift)
Route test asserts 200 + decoded body shape for a fixture event store

items.title: pan flywheel stats [--window 30d] CLI command

narrative.Action: In src/cli/commands/flywheel.ts register a new subcommand `stats` with option --window <duration> (default 30d). Implementation calls GET /api/flywheel/stats?window=<X> via the dashboard's internal-token client, prints a colored ASCII table with rows for each of 7 criteria: label | value | target | status (green/yellow/red glyph) | trend | sampleSize. Supports --json for raw output. Also update the pan-flywheel skill SKILL.md to document the new subcommand (per the SKILLS-CONVENTION rule).

subItems.title: `pan flywheel stats` prints 7-row table with default 30-day window
`pan flywheel stats --window 7d` queries with 7-day window
`pan flywheel stats --json` emits raw FlywheelStats JSON to stdout
Status column uses color (green/yellow/red) when stdout is a TTY; plain text when piped
skills/pan-flywheel/SKILL.md documents the `stats` subcommand with example output; scripts/lint-skills.sh passes
CLI test covers all three modes (default, --window, --json) with a mock dashboard

items.title: FlywheelStatsPanel React component rendering the 7 criteria cards

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/flywheel/FlywheelStatsPanel.tsx. Fetches GET /api/flywheel/stats on mount (and on a 60s interval). Renders 7 cards in a responsive grid: each card shows label, large value, target line, status indicator (colored dot + label), trend arrow if present, sampleSize footer. Insufficient-data state shows 'collecting since <date>' instead of a value. Tooltip on each card explains how the criterion is computed (per D12 / H9 notes). Component tests with fixture FlywheelStats.

subItems.title: Renders 7 cards, one per criterion, with label / value / target / status / trend / sampleSize
dataSufficient=false renders 'collecting since <date>' instead of a value, and status indicator shows neutral (not red)
Tooltip on each card explains the metric (taken from a constants map, not inline strings)
Auto-refreshes every 60s; handles fetch error gracefully (keeps last good data, shows banner)
Vitest component test with fixture FlywheelStats asserts all 7 cards render correctly
Accessibility: each card uses role=region with aria-label; status indicators have text labels in addition to color

items.title: Add Stats tab to FlywheelPage left pane

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/FlywheelPage.tsx extend FlywheelLeftTab type to 'status' | 'state' | 'stats'. Add a third tab button after State. When active, render <FlywheelStatsPanel /> in the tabpanel area. Preserve the existing Status / State tabs verbatim. Update FlywheelPage.test.tsx for the new tab.

subItems.title: Third tab labelled 'Stats' appears after 'State' with aria-selected wiring
Clicking Stats tab renders FlywheelStatsPanel; clicking Status / State switches back without losing fetched data
Existing Status and State tab behavior is preserved (verified by existing FlywheelPage tests still passing unmodified except for the new tab assertion)
Layout: stats panel does not crowd the primary right-pane outputs (per operator comment) — uses the existing left-pane split width

items.title: Document trailer convention in FLYWHEEL.md and flywheel-brief.md

narrative.Action: Add a 'Substrate-bug provenance' section to docs/FLYWHEEL.md explaining the trailer block, what each line means, when Filed-By is 'agent' vs 'operator', how the hook injects it, and how telemetry consumes it. Update docs/flywheel-brief.md to reference provenance as part of the substrate-bug filing workflow.

subItems.title: docs/FLYWHEEL.md has a 'Substrate-bug provenance' section with the trailer block example
docs/flywheel-brief.md references provenance rules in the substrate-bug section
Writing follows the clear-writing skill conventions (no AI-tells, no hedging)

items.title: Document the Stats tab + 7 criteria interpretation

narrative.Action: Add a 'Reading the Stats panel' section to docs/FLYWHEEL.md describing each of the 7 criteria, how it's computed, what status thresholds mean, and the 30-day window. Cross-link from FLYWHEEL-VISION.md v1.0 readiness criteria table to this doc (anchor link).

subItems.title: docs/FLYWHEEL.md has a 'Reading the Stats panel' section listing all 7 criteria with formula + threshold
FLYWHEEL-VISION.md v1.0 readiness table has anchor links to the new section
Lists the substrate-attributable heuristic (D13), complexity buckets (D12), flake definition (H9) so operators know what's measured

items.title: End-to-end smoke: file a fake substrate bug, verify trailer + projection + stats row

narrative.Action: Add an integration test (Vitest + a Playwright check) that: (1) simulates a Flywheel-orchestrator agent invoking `gh issue create` with PANOPTICON_FLYWHEEL_RUN_ID set — verifies trailer block is appended; (2) drives the substrate-bug poller against a fixture GitHub response containing the trailer — verifies the row appears in flywheel_substrate_bugs and SubstrateBugFiledEvent is emitted; (3) calls /api/flywheel/stats — verifies criterion 1 (bug-rate) value reflects the inserted bug; (4) Playwright opens FlywheelPage Stats tab in an isolated browser instance and asserts the criterion card renders the new value.

subItems.title: End-to-end test runs in CI and exercises all four stages (hook → poller → stats route → UI)
Playwright uses an isolated browser instance/profile (per Playwright Isolation rule); does not share state with other agents
Test uses vi.useFakeTimers + vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync for the poller delays — no real wall-clock waits
